content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA - HEALTH AND WELFARE AGENCY                    PETE WILSON, Governor

OFFICE OF STATEWIDE HEALTH PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT
CAL-MORTGAGE LOAN GUARANTEE PROGRAM
1600 - 9th Street, Room 400
Sacramento, CA 95814
(916) 324-9957                                April 4, 1991




Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814



Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     The Office of Statewide Health Planning and Development is
currently responsible for the administration of a program to
provide loan insurance for eligible health facility construction
loans. This program is found in Chapter 4 (commencing with section
436) and Part 1 of Division 1 of the Health and Safety Code.

     It is within the Cal-Mortgage Section of the Division of Facilities
Development and Finance that credit decisions are made regarding
short-term and long-term taxable and tax-exempt debt instruments,
including mortgages, certificates of participation, charter city
revenue bonds, and health facility revenue bond issues. A decision
to insure a project by the Office affects the relative credit
worthiness of a health facility construction project. A decision
to insure a project lends California's bond rating (AAA) to the
proposed debt instrument which results in a lower net interest cost
to the borrower than that which would have resulted, had the health
facility been rated (by Moody's or Standard and Poors) entirely on
its own credit.

     A decision to insure by this Office also results in the bond issue
being backed by the full faith and credit of the State of
California (see Health and Safety Code, section 436.20). In short,
a default of an insured mortgage of bond issues causes that debt
instrument to become a direct obligation of the State of
California, a situation we are not interested in having occur if we
can help it.

     The potential exposure of the State to losses as a result of the
default of an insured project requires that each application for
insurance be thoroughly examined in every aspect.

     In an effort to give each applicant close scrutiny for financial
viability, the Office has created, at 22 CCR 91499, and "Advisory
Loan Committee." The Committee is appointed by our Director. It
meets in public, in accordance with the State's open meeting law.
The text of our regulation establishes this committee as follows:

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
April 4, 1991
Page 2

91499. Advisory Loan Committee.
(a) The Committee shall advise the Office regarding the
borrower's financial capability to service the loan.
(b) The Committee shall be appointed by the Office and shall
consist of seven members as follows:
1. Five shall be appointed outside State
Government.
2. Two from the Office.
(c) The members shall be qualified in the field of financial
analysis of the operation of, capital outlay for, and
construction of health facilities.
(d) The members shall file statements of economic interest in
accordance with the provision of the Office's conflict of
interest code.
(e) No member may participate in any manner, including
voting, in the decision of the Committee regarding a
health facility in which such member has an economic
interest.
(f) Members appointed form outside State Government shall be
entitled to claim travel expenses equivalent to those
which State employees are entitled.

The current Advisory Loan Committee is made up of two government
members, one of whom is the Cal-Mortgage program manager and
chairperson, the other government member is an OSHPD architect.
The public members are appointed for five year terms and, by
profession, consist of one hospital administrator; two investment
bankers; and two Certified Public Accountants, one of whom is
employed by a hospital as the Financial Vice President and the
other is a practicing CPA. Each advisory member's professional
activities require daily interaction with the health industry and
nexus sometimes occurs in conjunction with agenda items.

We have made every effort to assure that a conflict of interest, or
even the appearance of a conflict, does not occur. I draw your
attention specially to subdivisions (d) and (e) of CCR Section
91497. Subdivision (d) requires the filing of conflict of interest
statements. Subdivision (e) precludes decision making or
participation in decision making where an actual conflict exists.

It is the policy of the Office and of the Advisory Loan Committee
that, when a conflict of interest or a potential conflict of
interest relating to a prospective action before the Committee is
recognized, the member or members with conflicting interest(s)
"step down" and do not participate in the decision making process
relative to that item. We know of no case where this has not
happened.

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
April 4, 1991
Page 3

When members "step down," they orally state the nature of their
conflicting interest(s).

The Office retains through an Inter-Agency Agreement with the State
Attorney General's office, an Assistant Attorney General who
attends loan committee meetings and advises the Office and the
Advisory Loan Committee regarding conflicts of interest.

The Office routinely advises the Advisory Loan Committee in writing
of public policy and any other official requirements. Enclosed
with this request you will find policy and information letters sent
to the Advisory Loan Committee.

The Office would appreciate an opinion from the Fair Political
Practices Commission as to the appropriateness and sufficiency of
the procedures described above, as they relate to precluding
conflicts of interest situations for Advisory Loan Committee
members and the Office. Do we comport with all state statutory
requirements for: 1) reporting financial interest so the public
can make its own informed judgment regarding conflicts; and 2)
prohibiting Advisory Loan Committee members from participating in
decision making, when an actual conflict exists?

If you have any questions, please call me directly at (916) 323-
6866. Thank you in advance for your help in this matter.

Sincerely,

Richard A. McManus
Chief, Cal-Mortgage Loan
Guarantee Program


Enclosures

State of California

Office of Statewide Health Planning and Development

CAL-MORTGAGE LOAN GUARANTEE PROGRAM

MEMORANDUM

TO: Loan Committee Members          DATE: March 28, 1991

FROM: Kathleen Azevedo             SUBJECT: Economic Interests


Please complete the Form 730, disclosure, and bring it with you to
the March 13, 1991 loan Committee Meeting in Sacramento. Sara will
be attending the meeting as Committee Secretary and will return
these forms to the Office.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

State of California                                Office of Statewide Health Planning and Development

M e m o r a n d u m


To      :   Richard McManus                                    Date  :  February 28, 1991
            Section Chief
            Division of Facilities Development and Financing        Disclosure  :  2, 5, 6,  &  7

From  :   Cindy A. Miner, Filing Officer[signature]
            Division of Administration
            1600 - 9th Street, Room 450
            Sacramento, CA  95814

Subject  :   Statement of Economic Interest
             "Annual Statement"

             The Political Reform Act of 1974 requires that officials and employees who are
             designated in the agency's conflict of interest code must file a Statement of
             Economic Interests Form 730. These statements disclose certain financial interests
             so the public may be aware of potential conflicts of interests. Within in this Office,
             your position is "designated" and the completion of a Form 730 is required. Your
             disclosure category is indicated above.

             Attached is a Statement of Economic Interests Manual and Form 730 .  The period
             covered by this annual statement is January 1, 1990 through December 31, 1990.

             Please complete the enclosed Form 730 and return it to my attention at the above
             address no later than  April 1, 1991.   Also attached is a copy of the office's
             disclosure categories which specifies the levels of disclosure.

             If you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 327-9052 or ATSS 8-467-9052.


             Attachments

STATE OF CALIFORNIA—HEALTH AND WELFARE AGENCY                    GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN Governor

OFFICE OF STATEWIDE HEALTH PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT
1600 9TH STREET
CRAMENTO CA 95814

(916) 324-9957


                                                    November 7, 1989


Joan Annett
Smith Barney, Harris Upham & Co.
350 California Street
San Francisco, CA  94115

Dear Joan:

     The Director has decided to restructure the Advisory Loan
Committee. His decision was based on the following observations:
(1) On average, only three of the five term members are able to
attend a given meeting and remain for the duration. (2)
Applications to the program have increased. (3) Meetings should be
held more frequently. (4) Members sometimes disqualify themselves
due to conflicts of interest. (5) There is a need for a broader
array of skills to improve the scope of review.

Here is the new structure:

     * There will be five term members, two State members, and two
       alternate members.

     * Each term member will be appointed to a five year term.

     * Alternate and State members will serve at the pleasure
       of the director and are not subject to term appointments.

     * Terms of term members shall be staggered so that each year
       one term will expire on December 31st.

     * Alternates are requested to attend each meeting and to fully
       participate in the discussion and critique, but will not
       be eligible to move, second, or vote.

     * Alternates may be appointed by the Chairman to replace an
       absent term or State member, in which case, the alternate
       member shall have full rights to move, second, and vote.

     * A majority of the permanent and government members consti-
       tutes a quorum.

     * Members may be replaced for excessive absenteeism.

Loan Committee Restructure
Page 2

* Passage of a motion requires a simple majority of members
voting.

* Meetings will be public and will be conducted in conformance
with the Keene-Bagley Act.

* Meetings will be scheduled monthly and may be canceled if
no projects have been received.

Term expiration dates have been established according to the date
of original appointment.

December 31, 1989 - Terry McCarty

December 31, 1990 - Vince Forte

December 31, 1991 - Joe Luevanos

December 31, 1992 - Joan Annett

December 31, 1993 - Niall Quinn

See you on December 6th.

Sincerely,

Richard A. McManus
Chief, Cal Mortgage

cc: Larry Meeks
Merrill Jacobs
Gary Pettigrew

Cal Mortgage Loan Program Advisory Loan Committee

Meeting Schedule and Application Filing Dates:

                                          Completed
                                          Applications
                                          Must be Re-
                Month  Day    Place        ceived by:

            1989:

                December  6    San Francisco        Nov 22
                              (May be changed)

            1990 (second Wednesday):

                January   10   Los Angeles          Dec 27

                February  14   San Francisco        Jan 31

                March     14   Sacramento           Feb 28

                April     11   Los Angeles          Mar 28

                May       9    San Francisco        Apr 25

                June      13   Sacramento           May 30

                July      11   Los Angeles          Jun 27

                August    8    San Francisco        Jul 25

                September 12   Sacramento           Aug 29

                October   10   Los Angeles          Sep 26

                November  14   San Francisco        Oct 31

                December  12   Los Angeles          Nov 28

7-6-89

Dear Vince:

The next loan committee meeting is tentatively set for Thursday,
August 17th. Time and place to be determined after the agenda is
known.

Our next meeting will be completely public with all discussions and
votes taken in the presence of applicants, consultants, and others
interested in our process. The meeting will be electronically
recorded and a secretary will be present.

Historically, committee members have been comfortable with an
informal meeting protocol resulting in a thorough review and frank
determination of financial feasibility. Through this process we
were able to protect proprietary information and probe project
weaknesses that might embarrass applicants if done publicly.

The Bagley-Keene Act, which governs state agency advisory committee
meetings of three or more persons, is specific in its application
to our loan committee. The attached Open Meeting Laws pamphlet,
published by the California Attorney General's office, is enclosed
for your reading. It conveniently summarizes the Act by issue with
cross references to specific code sections (pp3-6). This epistle
expedite your research and understanding. Please pay close
attention to the closed session exceptions (p4-5).

Here are some positive reasons why we should welcome this change to
a public forum:

* Its government in the best of democratic traditions.

* It will encourage public testimony from interested parties
(pro and con), thereby increasing your awareness of community
support and market conditions that may not be apparent in the
feasibility study.

* Applicants will be more precise in their assertions and
more conservative in their assumptions.

* Potential applicants and their consultants will have a
better understanding of what you as a loan committee member think
important and will respond by addressing these concerns in future
applications.

* Its the law.

I cannot envision that the public meeting environment will in any
way inhibit a thorough review of financial feasibility. You and
the other loan committee members are all experts in your field and
your opinions and criticisms are always relevant and on point.
Carol Hunter, Assistant Attorney General, has been invited to our
next meeting to answer any questions you may have about the Bagley-
Keene Act. In the meantime, call me if you have any questions or
concerns.

Sincerely,


_________________________________
Richard A. McManus
Chairman

Attachment
(for Joan and Naill indicate "Attachment - hand delivered")

cc  Larry
Gary
Merrill
Carol Hunter

sections.question_synthetic: Does the Cal-Mortgage Loan Guarantee Program's Advisory Loan Committee comply with state conflict of interest statutes regarding financial interest disclosure and recusal procedures for committee members?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter with detailed context documents attached (memoranda, restructuring letter, meeting schedule, and form instructions). No FPPC response conclusion is present in this document. The question section is clearly identified in the main letter from Richard McManus.

Do we comport with all state statutory requirements for: 1) reporting financial interest so the public can make its own informed judgment regarding conflicts, and 2) prohibiting Advisory Loan Committee members from participating in decision making, when an actual conflict exists?

embedding.summary: The California Office of Statewide Health Planning and Development requests FPPC advice on whether its Advisory Loan Committee's conflict of interest procedures comply with state statutory requirements for disclosure and recusal of members with economic interests. The request includes regulatory framework, committee composition, and supporting correspondence regarding conflict management practices.
